THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE HAS CONFIRMED THAT A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EASTERN WAYNE COUNTY, 1 MILE NORTH-NORTHEAST OF WALNUT CREEK AND NEAR AND ALONG HIGHWAY 70. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED TO THE NORTHWEST FOR APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE BEFORE LIFTING AND DISSIPATING. THE DAMAGE ALONG THIS PATH CONSISTED OF NUMEROUS DOWNED TREES, MOSTLY SOFTWOOD TREES THAT WERE SNAPPED, UPROOTED, AND BLOWN DOWN. SEVERAL BUILDINGS SUSTAINED RELATIVELY MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. AT LEAST ONE BILLBOARD WAS DESTROYED, AND A CAMPER WAS UPLIFTED AND ROTATED AROUND AN ADJACENT BUILDING, AND SUBSEQUENTLY SUSTAINED DAMAGE. SEVERAL FENCES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND BLOWN AWAY. THE DEGREE OF DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH SUGGESTED 80 MPH WIND SPEEDS (EF-0) ASSOCIATED WITH THIS TORNADO. THE NWS RALEIGH WOULD LIKE TO EXTEND THANKS AND APPRECIATION TO THE WAYNE COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE AND THE WAYNE COUNTY OFFICE OF EMERGENCY SERVICES FOR THEIR SUPPORT AND ASSISTANCE WITH THIS STORM SURVEY.
